CREATE TABLE myschema.mytable (id INT PRIMARY KEY, my_column INT);
CREATE ORDERED INDEX my_ord_idx ON myschema.mytable (my_column);
CREATE TABLE test_schema.test_table (age INT, name VARCHAR(100));
CREATE ORDERED INDEX age_index ON test_schema.test_table (age);
CREATE TABLE orders (order_id INT PRIMARY KEY, customer_id INT, order_date DATE);
CREATE ORDERED INDEX date_index ON orders (order_date);
CREATE TABLE products (product_id INT PRIMARY KEY, price DECIMAL(10, 2));
CREATE ORDERED INDEX price_index ON products (price);
CREATE TABLE people (user_id INT PRIMARY KEY, age INT, name VARCHAR(50));
INSERT INTO people (user_id, age, name) VALUES (1, 25, 'Alice'), (2, 30, 'Bob');
CREATE ORDERED INDEX age_idx ON people (age);
CREATE TABLE cars (car_id INT PRIMARY KEY, model VARCHAR(100), year INT);
CREATE ORDERED INDEX model_index ON cars (model);
CREATE ORDERED INDEX year_index ON cars (year);
CREATE TABLE sales (sales_id INT PRIMARY KEY, product_id INT, quantity INT);
CREATE ORDERED INDEX quantity_index ON sales (quantity);
CREATE ORDERED INDEX unique_index ON myschema.mytable (my_column);
INSERT INTO myschema.mytable (id, my_column) VALUES (1, 10), (2, 20);
DROP INDEX my_ord_idx;
CREATE ORDERED INDEX order_age_index ON test_schema.test_table(age);
CREATE ORDERED INDEX price_desc_index ON products(price);
SELECT * FROM products WHERE price > 100 ORDER BY price;
SELECT * FROM test_schema.test_table WHERE age >= 18 ORDER BY age;
